english_name: The Epistle of Paul the Apostle to the Romans

verses.latin_vulgate: Quid ergo amplius Judæo est? aut quæ utilitas circumcisionis?
verses.greek_textus_receptus: τί οὖν τὸ περισσὸν τοῦ ἰουδαίου ἢ τίς ἡ ὠφέλεια τῆς περιτομῆς
verses.king_james: What advantage then hath the Jew? or what profit *is there* of circumcision?

verses.latin_vulgate: Multum per omnem modum. Primum quidem quia credita sunt illis eloquia Dei.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: πολὺ κατὰ πάντα τρόπον πρῶτον μὲν γὰρ ὅτι ἐπιστεύθησαν τὰ λόγια τοῦ θεοῦ
verses.king_james: Much every way: chiefly, because that unto them were committed the oracles of God.

verses.latin_vulgate: Quid enim si quidam illorum non crediderunt? numquid incredulitas illorum fidem Dei evacuabit? Absit.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: τί γὰρ εἰ ἠπίστησάν τινες μὴ ἡ ἀπιστία αὐτῶν τὴν πίστιν τοῦ θεοῦ καταργήσει
verses.king_james: For what if some did not believe? shall their unbelief make the faith of God without effect?

verses.latin_vulgate: Est autem Deus verax: omnis autem homo mendax, sicut scriptum est: [Ut justificeris in sermonibus tuis: et vincas cum judicaris.]
verses.greek_textus_receptus: μὴ γένοιτο γινέσθω δὲ ὁ θεὸς ἀληθής πᾶς δὲ ἄνθρωπος ψεύστης καθὼς γέγραπται ὅπως ἂν δικαιωθῇς ἐν τοῖς λόγοις σου καὶ νικήσῃς ἐν τῷ κρίνεσθαί σε
verses.king_james: God forbid: yea, let God be true, but every man a liar; as it is written, That thou mightest be justified in thy sayings, and mightest overcome when thou art judged.

verses.latin_vulgate: Si autem iniquitas nostra justitiam Dei commendat, quid dicemus? Numquid iniquus est Deus, qui infert iram?
verses.greek_textus_receptus: εἰ δὲ ἡ ἀδικία ἡμῶν θεοῦ δικαιοσύνην συνίστησιν τί ἐροῦμεν μὴ ἄδικος ὁ θεὸς ὁ ἐπιφέρων τὴν ὀργήν κατὰ ἄνθρωπον λέγω
verses.king_james: But if our unrighteousness commend the righteousness of God, what shall we say? *Is* God unrighteous who taketh vengeance? (I speak as a man)

verses.latin_vulgate: secundum hominem dico. Absit. Alioquin quomodo judicabit Deus hunc mundum?
verses.greek_textus_receptus: μὴ γένοιτο ἐπεὶ πῶς κρινεῖ ὁ θεὸς τὸν κόσμον
verses.king_james: God forbid: for then how shall God judge the world?

verses.latin_vulgate: Si enim veritas Dei in meo mendacio abundavit in gloriam ipsius: quid adhuc et ego tamquam peccator judicor?
verses.greek_textus_receptus: εἰ γὰρ ἡ ἀλήθεια τοῦ θεοῦ ἐν τῷ ἐμῷ ψεύσματι ἐπερίσσευσεν εἰς τὴν δόξαν αὐτοῦ τί ἔτι κἀγὼ ὡς ἁμαρτωλὸς κρίνομαι
verses.king_james: For if the truth of God hath more abounded through my lie unto his glory; why yet am I also judged as a sinner?

verses.latin_vulgate: et non (sicut blasphemamur, et sicut aiunt quidam nos dicere) faciamus mala ut veniant bona: quorum damnatio justa est.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: καὶ μὴ καθὼς βλασφημούμεθα καὶ καθώς φασίν τινες ἡμᾶς λέγειν ὅτι ποιήσωμεν τὰ κακὰ ἵνα ἔλθῃ τὰ ἀγαθά ὧν τὸ κρίμα ἔνδικόν ἐστιν
verses.king_james: And not *rather*, (as we be slanderously reported, and as some affirm that we say,) Let us do evil, that good may come? whose damnation is just.

verses.latin_vulgate: Quid ergo? præcellimus eos? Nequaquam. Causati enim sumus Judæos et Græcos omnes sub peccato esse,
verses.greek_textus_receptus: τί οὖν προεχόμεθα οὐ πάντως προῃτιασάμεθα γὰρ ἰουδαίους τε καὶ ἕλληνας πάντας ὑφ᾽ ἁμαρτίαν εἶναι
verses.king_james: What then? are we better *than they* ? No, in no wise: for we have before proved both Jews and Gentiles, that they are all under sin;

verses.latin_vulgate: sicut scriptum est: [Quia non est justus quisquam:
verses.greek_textus_receptus: καθὼς γέγραπται ὅτι οὐκ ἔστιν δίκαιος οὐδὲ εἷς
verses.king_james: As it is written, There is none righteous, no, not one:

verses.latin_vulgate: non est intelligens, non est requirens Deum.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: οὐκ ἔστιν ὁ συνίων οὐκ ἔστιν ὁ ἐκζητῶν τὸν θεόν
verses.king_james: There is none that understandeth, there is none that seeketh after God.

verses.latin_vulgate: Omnes declinaverunt, simul inutiles facti sunt: non est qui faciat bonum, non est usque ad unum.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: πάντες ἐξέκλιναν ἅμα ἠχρειώθησαν οὐκ ἔστιν ποιῶν χρηστότητα οὐκ ἔστιν ἕως ἑνός
verses.king_james: They are all gone out of the way, they are together become unprofitable; there is none that doeth good, no, not one.

verses.latin_vulgate: Sepulchrum patens est guttur eorum, linguis suis dolose agebant: venenum aspidum sub labiis eorum:
verses.greek_textus_receptus: τάφος ἀνεῳγμένος ὁ λάρυγξ αὐτῶν ταῖς γλώσσαις αὐτῶν ἐδολιοῦσαν ἰὸς ἀσπίδων ὑπὸ τὰ χείλη αὐτῶν
verses.king_james: Their throat *is* an open sepulchre; with their tongues they have used deceit; the poison of asps *is* under their lips:

verses.latin_vulgate: quorum os maledictione, et amaritudine plenum est:
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ὧν τὸ στόμα ἀρᾶς καὶ πικρίας γέμει
verses.king_james: Whose mouth *is* full of cursing and bitterness:

verses.latin_vulgate: veloces pedes eorum ad effundendum sanguinem:
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ὀξεῖς οἱ πόδες αὐτῶν ἐκχέαι αἷμα
verses.king_james: Their feet *are* swift to shed blood:

verses.latin_vulgate: contritio et infelicitas in viis eorum:
verses.greek_textus_receptus: σύντριμμα καὶ ταλαιπωρία ἐν ταῖς ὁδοῖς αὐτῶν
verses.king_james: Destruction and misery *are* in their ways:

verses.latin_vulgate: et viam pacis non cognoverunt:
verses.greek_textus_receptus: καὶ ὁδὸν εἰρήνης οὐκ ἔγνωσαν
verses.king_james: And the way of peace have they not known:

verses.latin_vulgate: non est timor Dei ante oculos eorum.]
verses.greek_textus_receptus: οὐκ ἔστιν φόβος θεοῦ ἀπέναντι τῶν ὀφθαλμῶν αὐτῶν
verses.king_james: There is no fear of God before their eyes.

verses.latin_vulgate: Scimus autem quoniam quæcumque lex loquitur, iis, qui in lege sunt, loquitur: ut omne os obstruatur, et subditus fiat omnis mundus Deo:
verses.greek_textus_receptus: οἴδαμεν δὲ ὅτι ὅσα ὁ νόμος λέγει τοῖς ἐν τῷ νόμῳ λαλεῖ ἵνα πᾶν στόμα φραγῇ καὶ ὑπόδικος γένηται πᾶς ὁ κόσμος τῷ θεῷ
verses.king_james: Now we know that what things soever the law saith, it saith to them who are under the law: that every mouth may be stopped, and all the world may become guilty before God.

verses.latin_vulgate: quia ex operibus legis non justificabitur omnis caro coram illo. Per legem enim cognitio peccati.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: διότι ἐξ ἔργων νόμου οὐ δικαιωθήσεται πᾶσα σὰρξ ἐνώπιον αὐτοῦ διὰ γὰρ νόμου ἐπίγνωσις ἁμαρτίας
verses.king_james: Therefore by the deeds of the law there shall no flesh be justified in his sight: for by the law *is* the knowledge of sin.

verses.latin_vulgate: Nunc autem sine lege justitia Dei manifestata est: testificata a lege et prophetis.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: νυνὶ δὲ χωρὶς νόμου δικαιοσύνη θεοῦ πεφανέρωται μαρτυρουμένη ὑπὸ τοῦ νόμου καὶ τῶν προφητῶν
verses.king_james: But now the righteousness of God without the law is manifested, being witnessed by the law and the prophets;

verses.latin_vulgate: Justitia autem Dei per fidem Jesu Christi in omnes et super omnes qui credunt in eum: non enim est distinctio:
verses.greek_textus_receptus: δικαιοσύνη δὲ θεοῦ διὰ πίστεως ἰησοῦ χριστοῦ εἰς πάντας καὶ ἐπὶ πάντας τοὺς πιστεύοντας οὐ γάρ ἐστιν διαστολή
verses.king_james: Even the righteousness of God *which is* by faith of Jesus Christ unto all and upon all them that believe: for there is no difference:

verses.latin_vulgate: omnes enim peccaverunt, et egent gloria Dei.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: πάντες γὰρ ἥμαρτον καὶ ὑστεροῦνται τῆς δόξης τοῦ θεοῦ
verses.king_james: For all have sinned, and come short of the glory of God;

verses.latin_vulgate: Justificati gratis per gratiam ipsius, per redemptionem quæ est in Christo Jesu,
verses.greek_textus_receptus: δικαιούμενοι δωρεὰν τῇ αὐτοῦ χάριτι διὰ τῆς ἀπολυτρώσεως τῆς ἐν χριστῷ ἰησοῦ
verses.king_james: Being justified freely by his grace through the redemption that is in Christ Jesus:

verses.latin_vulgate: quem proposuit Deus propitiationem per fidem in sanguine ipsius, ad ostensionem justitiæ suæ propter remissionem præcedentium delictorum
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ὃν προέθετο ὁ θεὸς ἱλαστήριον διὰ τῆς πίστεως ἐν τῷ αὐτοῦ αἵματι εἰς ἔνδειξιν τῆς δικαιοσύνης αὐτοῦ διὰ τὴν πάρεσιν τῶν προγεγονότων ἁμαρτημάτων
verses.king_james: Whom God hath set forth *to be* a propitiation through faith in his blood, to declare his righteousness for the remission of sins that are past, through the forbearance of God;

verses.latin_vulgate: in sustentatione Dei, ad ostensionem justitiæ ejus in hoc tempore: ut sit ipse justus, et justificans eum, qui est ex fide Jesu Christi.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ἐν τῇ ἀνοχῇ τοῦ θεοῦ πρὸς ἔνδειξιν τῆς δικαιοσύνης αὐτοῦ ἐν τῷ νῦν καιρῷ εἰς τὸ εἶναι αὐτὸν δίκαιον καὶ δικαιοῦντα τὸν ἐκ πίστεως ἰησοῦ
verses.king_james: To declare, *I say*, at this time his righteousness: that he might be just, and the justifier of him which believeth in Jesus.

verses.latin_vulgate: Ubi est ergo gloriatio tua? Exclusa est. Per quam legem? Factorum? Non: sed per legem fidei.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ποῦ οὖν ἡ καύχησις ἐξεκλείσθη διὰ ποίου νόμου τῶν ἔργων οὐχί ἀλλὰ διὰ νόμου πίστεως
verses.king_james: Where *is* boasting then? It is excluded. By what law? of works? Nay: but by the law of faith.

verses.latin_vulgate: Arbitramur enim justificari hominem per fidem sine operibus legis.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: λογιζόμεθα οὖν πίστει δικαιοῦσθαι ἄνθρωπον χωρὶς ἔργων νόμου
verses.king_james: Therefore we conclude that a man is justified by faith without the deeds of the law.

verses.latin_vulgate: An Judæorum Deus tantum? nonne et gentium? Immo et gentium:
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ἢ ἰουδαίων ὁ θεὸς μόνον οὐχὶ δὲ καὶ ἐθνῶν ναὶ καὶ ἐθνῶν
verses.king_james: *Is he* the God of the Jews only? *is he* not also of the Gentiles? Yes, of the Gentiles also:

verses.latin_vulgate: quoniam quidem unus est Deus, qui justificat circumcisionem ex fide, et præputium per fidem.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ἐπείπερ εἷς ὁ θεός ὃς δικαιώσει περιτομὴν ἐκ πίστεως καὶ ἀκροβυστίαν διὰ τῆς πίστεως
verses.king_james: Seeing *it is* one God, which shall justify the circumcision by faith, and uncircumcision through faith.

verses.latin_vulgate: Legem ergo destruimus per fidem? Absit: sed legem statuimus.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: νόμον οὖν καταργοῦμεν διὰ τῆς πίστεως μὴ γένοιτο ἀλλὰ νόμον ἱστῶμεν
verses.king_james: Do we then make void the law through faith? God forbid: yea, we establish the law.
